title: Communication apparatus and transmission method for transmitting a demodulation reference signal
abstract: A repetition unit (212) performs a repetition for mapping a data signal and a demodulation reference signal (DMRS) repeatedly at a symbol level over a plurality of subframes. A signal allocation unit (213) maps, in the a plurality of subframes, the repeated DMRS to symbols other than symbols corresponding to an SRS resource candidate, which is a candidate for a resource to which a sounding reference signal (SRS) to be used to measure an uplink received signal quality is to be mapped. A transmission unit (216) transmits an uplink signal (PUSCH) including the DMRS and the data signal over the a plurality of subframes.
claims: 1. A communication apparatus, comprising: a receiver, which, in operation, receives control information indicating a number of symbols on which a Demodulation Reference Signal (DMRS) is to be mapped; and a transmitter, which, in operation, transmits a signal in a multiple consecutive time units, each of the multiple consecutive time units including multiple time slots; wherein the DMRS is mapped to each of multiple non-consecutive sets in the multiple consecutive time units, a number of symbols in each of the multiple non-consecutive sets being determined based on the control information, the number of symbols of each of the multiple non-consecutive sets is less than 14 symbols regardless of a number of the multiple consecutive time units, the symbols of each of the multiple non-consecutive sets are symbols other than sounding reference signal (SRS) symbol candidates, and a number of the multiple consecutive time units is different for each of numbers of subcarriers to be mapped.
